summary: The test suite comprehensively covers the core components (manifest parsing, license lookup, compliance checking, report generation) with 21 focused unit tests organized into logical classes. Strengths: clear test names, proper isolation with tempfile, independent tests, good error handling. Weaknesses: missing edge cases (!=operator in requirements.txt, bare package names, overlapping allow/deny lists), no CLI/main() function tests, no integration tests with fixture files, repeated code patterns that could use pytest fixtures. The suite provides solid unit test coverage but lacks end-to-end testing and several edge case scenarios that real dependency data would exercise.
